---
title: "Random Randomness"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
latest_refresh_datetime <- format(Sys.time(), tz = "Pacific/Auckland")
```

Latest refresh at `r latest_refresh_datetime` NZST.

> This book is all about random randomness I found. Not organised in my brain yet. Dump everything here for the ease of reference.

# HAPPY CODING

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

files <- sort(list.files("random-files/coding-related/", pattern = "\\.md$", full.names = TRUE))
for (f in files) {
  name <- basename(f)
  display <- sub("\\.md$", "", name)
  cat(sprintf("- [%s](%s)\n", display, f))
}
```

# LOOK AFTER HEALTH

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

files <- sort(list.files("random-files/health-related/", pattern = "\\.md$", full.names = TRUE))
for (f in files) {
  name <- basename(f)
  display <- sub("\\.md$", "", name)
  cat(sprintf("- [%s](%s)\n", display, f))
}
```

# ALL OTHER STUFF

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

files <- sort(list.files("random-files/other/", pattern = "\\.md$", full.names = TRUE))
for (f in files) {
  name <- basename(f)
  display <- sub("\\.md$", "", name)
  cat(sprintf("- [%s](%s)\n", display, f))
}
```

# ALREADY READ

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| eval: false

files <- sort(list.files("random-files/already-read/", pattern = "\\.md$", full.names = TRUE))
for (f in files) {
  name <- basename(f)
  display <- sub("\\.md$", "", name)
  cat(sprintf("- [%s](%s)\n", display, f))
}
```
